payload.content: Use JWT tokens with 1-hour expiry for session authentication. Refresh tokens are stored server-side and rotated on each use.
All unit tests use table-driven test patterns in Go. Each test function covers a single behaviour and uses t.Run for subtests.
Database connections use a connection pool with max 20 connections. Queries timeout after 5 seconds.
Cache API responses in Redis with 5-minute TTL to reduce latency. Use cache-aside pattern and invalidate on write.
Code formatting follows gofmt conventions. All exported functions must have a doc comment.
The web scraper uses Puppeteer for dynamic pages and cheerio for static HTML. Rate limiting is set to 1 request per second.
Use Markdown extraction when scraping documentation pages. Convert all code blocks to properly fenced markdown blocks.

payload.name: auth-handler
payload.description: Handles JWT token creation, validation, and refresh for user authentication flows.

payload.name: semantic-search
payload.description: Hybrid vector and keyword search for knowledge retrieval with re-ranking.

payload.name: db-migrator
payload.description: Manages database schema migrations, connection pooling, and query timeouts.

payload.name: Backend Architecture
payload.abstract: Core backend services including API gateway, authentication middleware, and database layer.
payload.content: The backend is structured around three layers: API gateway handles routing and rate limiting, auth middleware validates JWT tokens, and the data layer uses SQLite with a connection pool.

payload.name: Auth Module
payload.abstract: JWT-based authentication using RSA-256 signatures with refresh token rotation.
payload.content: Tokens are issued with a 1-hour expiry. Refresh tokens are stored in a server-side store keyed by user ID and rotated on each use to prevent replay attacks.

cases.query: web page scraping dynamic content puppeteer markdown
